questions.question: In law, a child is defined as anyone who has not yet reached their:
Which Act introduced the principle that a child's welfare is the paramount consideration when a court decides questions about their upbringing?
Which Act strengthened multi-agency working and placed a duty on organisations to co-operate to improve children's wellbeing?
Persistent maltreatment such as constant criticism, humiliation, threats, or making a child feel worthless or unloved describes which category of abuse?
Deliberately fabricating the symptoms of an illness in a child is an example of which category of abuse?
The ongoing failure to meet a child's basic physical or psychological needs, such as food, shelter, supervision or emotional warmth, describes which category of abuse?
Forcing or enticing a child to take part in sexual activities, whether or not the child is aware of what is happening, describes which category of abuse?
Because there is no fixed legal definition of 'significant' harm, practitioners weigh up its severity, duration and frequency, and its impact on:
Support to a 'child in need' under section 17 of the Children Act 1989 is offered:
options: on a compulsory basis by court order
on a voluntary basis with the family

questions.question: In an emergency where a child is in immediate danger, who should be contacted?
Which legislation allows you to share information in order to safeguard a child?
options: The Data Protection Act 2018 and UK GDPR

questions.question: A Multi-Agency Safeguarding Hub (MASH) is best described as:
options: a national database of all children
a hub where the local authority, police, health and other partners share information about a child
a team that only investigates adult abuse

questions.question: Child sexual exploitation (CSE) is best described as:
options: the neglect of a child's medical needs
a voluntary early help service for families
a form of sexual abuse where a child is manipulated or coerced into sexual activity in exchange for something such as money, gifts, affection or status

questions.question: Prevent is best described as:
options: a type of early help assessment
the part of the Government's counter-terrorism strategy that aims to stop people being drawn into terrorism
a child protection enquiry under section 47

questions.question: There is a fixed legal definition of 'significant harm' set out in the Children Act 1989.
Fear of breaching confidentiality or data protection law is a valid reason to leave a child at risk without sharing information.
Some level of emotional abuse is present in all types of child maltreatment.
A child may experience more than one category of abuse at the same time, and abuse can be inflicted by an adult, another child, or a group.
Which of the following can be possible indicators of physical abuse? (Select all that apply)
options: Injuries that always appear only on the hands
A child who is meeting all developmental milestones
A child who flinches at contact
Bruises in the shape of a hand or an object

questions.question: Which of the following can be possible signs of emotional or sexual abuse? (Select all that apply)
options: A child consistently excelling at school, proving they are safe
A child who is unusually confident with every adult
A child who seems fearful of a parent
Sexual knowledge or behaviour beyond what is expected for the child's age
A child who is withdrawn or overly anxious

questions.question: Which of the following can be possible signs of child criminal exploitation or 'county lines'? (Select all that apply)
Which of the following are principles of sharing information well to safeguard a child? (Select all that apply)
options: Share what is necessary and proportionate with those who need it
Record what you shared and why
Withhold information to protect the family's privacy even when a child may be at risk
Normally be open and honest with the family, unless doing so would put the child at greater risk

questions.question: When sharing information to safeguard a child, you should share only what is necessary and ___, with those who need it.
Child protection is the part of safeguarding that responds when a child is suffering, or is likely to suffer, ___.
Many areas run a Multi-Agency Safeguarding Hub, commonly abbreviated as ___, where partners share information to build a fuller picture of a child.
